symptom: NCCL Ring algorithm deadlock on A100 clusters with >32 nodes
trigger_field: nccl_algo + nccl_version + gpu_type + nodes
notes: Quarantined from the old flat rules.py (NCCL-RING-001), which had no source_url at all. Re-mining attempt 1 (this session): searched NVIDIA/nccl and nccl-tests issues for 'Ring algorithm deadlock A100', 'Ring deadlock A100', node-count-threshold hangs, etc. Found nothing supporting this specific claim (Ring algo + A100 + >32 nodes + fixed at 2.21) - the closest hits were an unrelated, still-open, unexplained nccl-tests hang at 18 nodes (no version tie, no root cause) and a closed-completed issue about a different topic (dummy GPU + NCCL_LAUNCH_MODE=GROUP). Kept at needs_remining rather than moved to rejected, since 'rejected' should mean a specific source was found that refutes/contradicts the claim, not just an unsuccessful search - but treat this as effectively dead unless a genuinely new lead (different query, different source) turns up; do not keep re-running the same searches.

symptom: AllReduce data corruption on H100 when NIC shares PCI switch with one GPU
trigger_field: nccl_version + gpu_type
notes: Promoted to rules/version_incompat.py as NCCL-H100-001 (WARN). Actual failure additionally requires a specific PCI topology (a NIC sharing a PCI switch with exactly one GPU) that traincheck cannot detect statically - if promoted, should be WARN with a topology caveat in the message, not a hard ERROR. Workaround per NVIDIA: NCCL_PROTO=^LL128 or NCCL_IB_PCI_RELAXED_ORDERING=0.

symptom: DistBackendError NCCL error with PyTorch Lightning DDP/DP strategy
trigger_field: nccl_version + framework_version
notes: SKIPPED at extraction, recorded only for the audit trail: thread has multiple conflicting unconfirmed theories (pytorch 2.1.0 regression, CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES ordering, an unrelated USB-NIC bug fixed in 2.20.x per #1125), no linked fix commit, and was auto-closed by a stale-issue bot (not 'completed'). Does not meet the closed-with-linked-fix bar. Do not re-mine without a clearer, single-cause report.

symptom: ZeRO-3 + PEFT LoRA bf16 base model: TypeError dtype mismatch in _allgather_params_coalesced

fixed_in.version: unreleased as of mining date - merged to main via PR #8073 (commit b5b3fded), no tagged release after 0.19.2 yet contains it

trigger_field: sharding (ZeRO stage) + peft/lora usage (not tracked) + bf16 enabled (not tracked)
notes: Real, well-documented regression with a merged fix PR (#8073, confirmed merged=true via API) - but traincheck has no PEFT/LoRA usage extraction at all, and no field for a DeepSpeed config's own bf16.enabled flag, so the trigger can't be evaluated even though sharding (ZeRO stage 3) alone is visible. Confidence capped at medium rather than high: the fix is merged to main but not yet in any tagged release as of the mining date, so there is no 'upgrade to X' remediation to give a user yet. Re-mine once a release ships and/or if PEFT/LoRA detection is ever added.

symptom: FSDP2 mixed-precision fp32 upcast is a silent no-op, trains in bf16 master weights
trigger_field: mixed_precision + accelerate package version (now tracked via dependency_constraints, wired into every adapter this session)
notes: PROMOTED to rules/version_incompat.py as ACCELERATE-FSDP2-001. RE-VERIFIED after this session's field-gap fix: dependency_constraints is now populated by extract_lockfile() in every adapter (bare/hpc_shell/skypilot/k8s), and accelerate was added to the tracked-package set. mixed_precision != 'no' and parse_pinned_version(dependency_constraint(dependency_constraints, 'accelerate')) == (1,13,0) is now a real, expressible condition - both sides are populated JobSpec data. The GitHub issue itself was closed by a stale-bot with an unanswered 'is this fixed?' comment - NOT sufficient alone. Verified independently by diffing accelerate's own source history: buggy `param = param.to(torch.float32)` introduced by PR #3905 (merged 2026-01-13, first in v1.13.0) was reverted to the correct `param.data = param.data.to(torch.float32)` by PR #3985 (merged 2026-04-23, first in v1.14.0), which also added a regression test asserting fp32 master weights. Bug is real, silent (no error, no crash - just wrong-precision training), and precisely bounded to exactly accelerate==1.13.0. Not expressible: traincheck has no field for the accelerate package's own pip-installed version (only framework_version/framework_name for the training framework, and dependency_constraints which is only wired for Ray). Would become directly expressible (mixed_precision != 'no' and accelerate_version == (1,13,0)) if an accelerate-version field were added, e.g. parsed from a requirements.txt/lockfile pin the way Ray's dependency_constraints already works.

symptom: Hang on GB200/300 with CX8 NIC when GDR is disabled
trigger_field: nccl_version + gpu_type + nccl_net_gdr_level (comm_env)
notes: PROMOTED to rules/version_incompat.py as NCCL-GB200-001. 2.29.2's own release notes list 'Fixed a hang on GB200/300 with CX8 when GDR is disabled' as a fixed issue, confirming it existed in some earlier version - but neither 2.28.7 nor 2.28.9 (the two immediately preceding tagged releases checked) document it as a known issue, so the exact start of the broken range is unconfirmed (capped confidence at medium instead of high for this reason, not because the fix itself is in doubt). Also needs NCCL_IB_HCA/CX8 NIC model detection (not currently a distinguishable comm_env value - CX8 isn't identified from any tracked var) to fully match the trigger; as written the condition can only check nccl_version + gpu_type + GDR-disabled, which is necessary but not sufficient (NIC model can't be verified). Do not promote without narrowing the start version further or accepting the coarser trigger with a caveat in the message. PROMPT-B: re-fetched 2.29.2 directly, exact wording re-confirmed ('Fixed a hang on GB200/300 + CX8 when the user disables GDR.'). Checked 2.28.9 for an earlier appearance as known issue - not present/no Known Issues section on that page - start of broken range still unconfirmed, kept at medium. No falsifying report found. Verified.

symptom: Message truncated error on NCCL 2.20.5 H100 cluster, absent on 2.18.5
trigger_field: nccl_version + gpu_type
notes: SKIPPED at extraction, recorded for the audit trail: closed as not_planned (not completed/fixed) by a stale-issue bot. Maintainer only offered a hedged guess ('we fixed a similar-looking bug in 2.21, can you try?'), then walked that back ('actually I'm not sure upgrading will help'). Reporter never confirmed resolution. Does not meet the closed-with-confirmed-fix bar despite naming two specific versions.

symptom: Host memory leak when reusing communicators across repeated collective calls

fixed_in.version: some release after 2.23.4 (exact tag not independently confirmed)

trigger_field: nccl_version + NCCL profiler plugin usage (not tracked)
notes: Real, maintainer-confirmed leak (NVIDIA's gcongiu identified it, shared a patch named nccl_v2.23_profiler_memleak.patch, and the reporter independently confirmed the patch fixes it: 'I checked the patch and it fixes a leak'). Confidence capped at medium, not high: checked NCCL 2.24.3 and 2.25.1's own official release notes and neither explicitly names this fix, so the exact shipped tagged version is unconfirmed (may be folded into a generic 'various bug fixes' entry). Not expressible regardless: the leak is specific to NCCL's profiler plugin API, which traincheck has no way to detect a job is using at all.

symptom: cuBLAS init fails on Hopper GPUs under MPS with thread percentage below 100

fixed_in.version: cuBLAS 12.3 Update 1 (confirmed resolved by CUDA 12.4's own release notes)

trigger_field: cuda_version + gpu_type (MPS usage itself is not tracked)
notes: PROMOTED to rules/version_incompat.py as CUBLAS-HOPPER-MPS-001. Confirmed on two independent CUDA release-notes pages: 12.2.1 lists it as a known issue verbatim, 12.4.0 lists the exact same wording under Resolved Issues attributed to 'cuBLAS 12.3 Update 1'. Like NCCL-H100-001, the checkable part (cuda_version + gpu_type=Hopper/H100) is a necessary but not sufficient condition - MPS (Multi-Process Service) usage isn't tracked by traincheck at all, so promote as WARN with an explicit 'only applies if using MPS' caveat, not a hard ERROR. PROMPT-B: re-fetched 12.2.1 (bug) and 12.4.0 directly. 12.4.0's dedicated 'cuBLAS: Release 12.3 Update 1 > Resolved Issues' section explicitly states 'Fixed creation of cuBLAS or cuBLASLt handles on Hopper GPUs under the Multi-Process Service (MPS)' - this is a stronger, more direct fix-credit than previously cited (previously only inferred from wording match, now confirmed via the page's own Resolved Issues heading). Confidence remains high. No falsifying report found. Verified.

symptom: Silent incorrect results for Hopper batched matmul with RELU_BIAS or GELU_BIAS epilogue
trigger_field: cuda_version + gpu_type (specific cuBLASLt epilogue usage is not tracked)
notes: PROMOTED to rules/version_incompat.py as CUBLAS-HOPPER-EPILOGUE-001. PROMPT-B CORRECTION: the original extraction pass wrongly claimed this was 'confirmed present... in 12.8.0's release notes, still open/unresolved.' Re-fetched 12.8.0 directly (raw HTML, not a summarized fetch) and that claim was false - CUDA's archive pages are cumulative changelogs that reprint every past release's own historical Known Issues verbatim under a dated heading (e.g. '2.1.x cuBLAS: Release 12.2'), so finding this text anywhere in the 12.8.0 page is NOT evidence it's still broken as of 12.8.0 - it's just historical record-keeping. Checked 12.8.0's own live Known Issues section (the un-dated current one): this bug is not in it. Also checked 12.3 Update 1's own 'Resolved Issues' section (which DOES explicitly credit fixing the neighboring MPS-init bug and 3 other cuBLAS bugs) - it does NOT mention this RELU_BIAS/GELU_BIAS bug, so no explicit fix credit exists either. Net: real bug, confirmed present as of cuBLAS 12.2 (source_url + 12.4.0 corroboration both hold up under re-check), but whether/when it was fixed is genuinely unconfirmed either way (dropped from the live list with no resolved-issues note - could be silently fixed or could be an editorial omission). Kept at medium confidence, version_range left open-ended (no upper bound asserted), same practical WARN promotion pattern as before - this correction changes the citation trail, not the rule content.

symptom: mma.sp PTX sparsity feature intermittent silent data corruption on Hopper
trigger_field: cuda_version + gpu_type (custom mma.sp PTX kernel usage is not tracked)
notes: PROMOTED to rules/version_incompat.py as CUDA-HOPPER-MMASP-001. Confirmed verbatim present as a known issue in CUDA 12.3.2's release notes (silent data corruption, NVIDIA's own libraries don't use this PTX instruction so only custom kernels calling it directly are affected). No longer appears in 12.6.0 or 12.8.0's release notes, but neither page has an explicit 'resolved'/'fixed' note crediting a fix for it either - absence from the list is not the same as a confirmed fix, so fixed_in is left null and the upper bound of the range is capped at the last version where I could still literally see it stated (12.3.2), not extended further on the assumption its disappearance means resolution. Re-check a 12.4/12.5 page directly before promoting to pin down whether/when it was actually fixed. PROMPT-B: re-fetched 12.3.2 directly, exact wording re-confirmed. Re-confirmed absence from 12.8.0's raw HTML (grepped for 'mma.sp' directly, zero matches) - consistent with the original notes' own caution not to assume this means fixed. No resolved-issues credit found anywhere. Confidence remains medium, range stays capped at <=12.3.2 as originally scoped. Verified.

symptom: CUDA 12.x requires driver >=525.60.13 (Linux) or >=528.33 (Windows) for minor-version compatibility
trigger_field: cuda_version + driver_version (host-dependent, live probe only)
notes: PROMOTED to rules/version_incompat.py as CUDA12X-DRIVER-001 (needs-verification advisory - only evaluates once driver_version is resolved via --probe-host; otherwise the engine's existing unknown-status routing surfaces it in needs_verification, not as a hard pass/fail). Not a 'bug' in the incident-report sense - this is NVIDIA's own published minimum-driver-version requirement table for CUDA 12.x minor-version compatibility (Table 2 in the release notes). Genuinely authoritative and precise, but driver_version is host_dependent (only known live via --probe-host), so per discipline #5 this should convert to a needs-verification advisory rather than a hard ERROR/WARN - it can only fire meaningfully when probe-host data is available. PROMPT-B: re-fetched 12.8.0 directly, Table 2 values re-confirmed exactly (>=525.60.13 Linux, >=528.33 Windows). This is a vendor compatibility table, not an incident report - nothing to falsify. Still host_dependent; the open design question of how to implement a host-dependent needs-verification rule in code remains unresolved. Verified.

symptom: ncclCommAbort hangs on process group teardown with NCCL 2.25.1
trigger_field: nccl_version
notes: PROMOTED to rules/version_incompat.py as NCCL-2251-001. Reporter identified the exact fix version first-hand in the original report ('fixes with NCCL 2.26.2-1 which released yesterday'), a fix PR (#149351) was cherry-picked into the PyTorch 2.7 release branch to bring in the NCCL upgrade, and a maintainer confirmed 'Fix has been applied, closing.' Cleanest, most precisely self-confirmed candidate found this session - single-sided (nccl_version alone, no gpu_type dependency stated in the primary report, though the loosely-related corroborating NVIDIA/nccl#1637 suggests RTX 5090/Blackwell may be a factor - not confirmed exclusive to it, so not added as a second condition). Promotable as ERROR: a hang on teardown is unconditional within this exact version, no untracked precondition needed. PROMPT-B: re-fetched the PyTorch issue directly, re-confirmed the NCCL 2.25.1-1 hang and the NCCL 2.26.2-1 fix. Pulled the full comment thread: reopened specifically to cherry-pick into PyTorch 2.7 (PR #149351), a comment adds 'NCCL 2.25.1 is completely broken with multiple 5090s' (reinforcing, not contradicting), and the thread closes with 'Fix has been applied, closing.' Falsification search ('NCCL 2.25.1 ncclCommAbort hang works fine') found only unrelated hang reports, nothing contradicting this specific fix. Confidence remains high. Verified - strongest promotion candidate in this batch.

symptom: NVLS transport CUDA invalid-argument failure when built against newer CUDA toolkit than installed driver
trigger_field: cuda_version + driver_version (host-dependent)
notes: SKIPPED at extraction, recorded for the audit trail: reporter built a precise pass/fail matrix (toolkit-vs-driver combinations) suggesting a driver-version threshold, but the thread's own resolution is inconsistent - a maintainer later attributed the fix to upgrading NCCL itself to 2.26.5, not to a driver requirement, and the reporter never confirmed whether that actually resolved it. Two different, unreconciled causal theories with no final confirmation - does not meet the bar.

symptom: ZeRO-2 training throughput regression introduced in 0.18.5
trigger_field: sharding (ZeRO stage 2) + deepspeed package version (now tracked via dependency_constraints)
notes: PROMOTED to rules/version_incompat.py as DEEPSPEED-ZERO2-001. RE-VERIFIED after this session's field-gap fix: deepspeed was already in the lockfile extractor's tracked-package set, but no adapter ever called extract_lockfile() at all - now wired in everywhere, so sharding==2 and a deepspeed pin in [0.18.5,0.18.7) is now a real, expressible condition. Cleanly confirmed: maintainer (tohtana) reproduced the slowdown, opened fix PR #7886, reporter cherry-picked it onto v0.18.6 and confirmed 'It works!' with before/after performance graphs, then maintainer confirmed v0.18.7 shipped the fix and closed the issue. A performance regression, not a crash - squarely in traincheck's own stated mission ('quietly tank your throughput'). Rejected only for the same expressibility gap as deepspeed-0192-zero3-peft-lora-dtype: no field tracks the DeepSpeed *package* version (only ds_config.json content like zero_stage). This is now the third finding blocked by that same gap (also accelerate's package version, x2) - re-mine and promote immediately if that field is ever added.

symptom: Hang on H100 with Ring algorithm and LL128 protocol on exactly 2 GPUs
trigger_field: nccl_version + gpu_type + nccl_algo + nodes*gpus_per_node
notes: PROMOTED to rules/version_incompat.py as NCCL-2155-001. NVIDIA's own Fixed Issues list for 2.15.5 names this verbatim: 'Workaround hang on H100 with Ring/LL128 on 2 GPUs' - implies broken in all prior releases (no earlier release note names an introduction version, so left open-ended as '<2.15.5' rather than inventing a lower bound). Note it says WORKAROUND, not a root fix - re-check later NCCL major versions if a true fix is ever named. Unlike the necessary-but-not-sufficient H100 candidates elsewhere in this file, all four trigger fields (nccl_version, gpu_type, nccl_algo, nodes*gpus_per_node==2) are real, already-populated JobSpec fields - this one is fully expressible, no untrackable precondition. The '2 total GPUs' constraint is narrow (most multi-node training jobs have far more), but a small dev/eval/debug job on a single H100 node with 2 GPUs is a real, plausible target. Single-source (no independent corroboration found via web search), but NVIDIA's own release notes qualify as authoritative per README.md discipline #4. PROMPT-B: re-fetched 2.15.5 directly, exact wording re-confirmed among the 7 Fixed Issues bullets. Falsification search for H100+Ring+LL128 hangs found only distinct, unrelated hang reports (different symptom signatures, no contradiction of this specific NVIDIA-documented fix). Confidence remains high. Verified.

sides.version_range: any build lacking torch.float8_e8m0fnu (notably NVIDIA NGC 2.7.0a0 container builds, even though the public 2.7.0 wheel has it)

symptom: AttributeError: module 'torch' has no attribute 'float8_e8m0fnu' on `from transformers import AutoProcessor`
trigger_field: transformers package version (not tracked) + torch build's FP8 dtype support (not reliably inferable from framework_version alone)
notes: Verified directly against source, not just the issue text: fetched src/transformers/integrations/finegrained_fp8.py from release tags v5.10.1/v5.10.2/v5.10.3 (all still have the unconditional module-level `_UE8M0_SF_DTYPE = torch.float8_e8m0fnu` at line 45, which crashes on import if torch lacks the attribute) versus v5.11.0 (guarded behind a `hasattr(torch, 'float8_e8m0fnu')` check with a clear error message, per merged PR #46393 'fix: compatibility with torch<=2.7'). Issue #46515 is the same crash independently reported by a second user. Rejected for two compounding reasons: (1) traincheck has no `transformers` package-version field at all (same gap as megatron/apex/accelerate/deepspeed elsewhere in this file); (2) even if it did, the real trigger isn't a clean torch version boundary - the reporter confirmed the *public* torch==2.7.0 CPU wheel has the dtype while NVIDIA's NGC-patched '2.7.0a0+...nv25.03' build (same nominal version) does not, so framework_version alone can't reliably predict this failure even if it were compared against a torch side. Would need both a transformers-version field AND a way to distinguish NGC container torch builds from the public wheel - a bigger lift than the other Tier 2 gaps.

symptom: AttributeError: 'Metadata' object has no attribute 'mcore_data' during Megatron-LM distributed checkpoint save/load
trigger_field: framework_version (torch) + Megatron-LM/mcore usage (now detectable via dependency_constraints['megatron-core'], added to the tracked-package set this session)
notes: PROMOTED to rules/version_incompat.py as MEGATRON-DCP-001. RE-VERIFIED after this session's field-gap fix: added 'megatron-core' to the lockfile extractor's tracked packages specifically to unblock this finding - its mere presence in dependency_constraints (any version) is a real Megatron/mcore-usage signal, addressing the 'no Megatron usage signal at all' half of the original gap. The other original caveat still applies: framework_version is single-purpose and may reflect a different named framework than torch - stated explicitly in the promoted rule's message. Proved the cause myself directly against source, not just the issue text: torch's torch/distributed/checkpoint/filesystem.py added `metadata = dataclasses.replace(metadata, version=CURRENT_DCP_VERSION)` in `finish()` - confirmed present at line 755 in both v2.9.0 and v2.9.1, still present (line 764) in v2.10.0, and confirmed ABSENT in v2.8.0 (which has no dataclasses.replace(metadata, ...) call at all). dataclasses.replace() drops any dynamically-added attribute (Python dataclass semantics), which strips Megatron-LM's custom `mcore_data` attribute on the DCP Metadata object, breaking checkpoint save/load for any Megatron/mcore-based trainer. Root PyTorch issue #162948 confirms this with a minimal dataclasses repro and explicitly names Megatron-LM as the affected downstream. PyTorch's own fix PR #162953 was closed WITHOUT merging - unresolved on the pytorch side as of mining date, so no upper bound / fixed_in exists yet; per README's confidence rule this caps at medium despite the unusually strong proof-of-cause, specifically because 'still open with no fix' caps confidence. Rejected for expressibility: traincheck has no Megatron-LM version field and no 'is this a Megatron/mcore training job' signal at all (only downstream parallelism knobs like sequence_parallel/expert_parallel/context_parallel that Megatron configs also set); framework_version is also single-purpose (whichever one framework a user names), so it can't simultaneously represent 'torch>=2.9' AND 'Megatron in use' even if Megatron detection existed. Re-mine if Megatron/mcore usage detection is ever added.

symptom: nvcc fatal: Unsupported gpu architecture 'compute_100' building apex GroupNorm on CUDA<12.8

fixed_in.version: merged to main via PR #1927 (2025-10-03); no tagged apex release since 25.09 contains it yet

trigger_field: cuda_version + apex usage (now detectable via dependency_constraints - presence alone is enough, exact version not required for this bug)
notes: PROMOTED to rules/version_incompat.py as APEX-GROUPNORM-001. RE-VERIFIED after this session's field-gap fix: apex was already tracked by the lockfile extractor but never reachable since no adapter called it - now wired in. This bug doesn't depend on apex's own version, only on cuda_version + apex being used at all, so mere presence in dependency_constraints is sufficient to make this expressible. setup.py's group_norm_v2 CUDAExtension unconditionally requested sm_100/sm_120 architecture flags regardless of the CUDA toolkit actually in use, breaking the build on CUDA 12.4-12.7 with 'nvcc fatal: Unsupported gpu architecture compute_100'. Confirmed by NVIDIA engineer @ptrblck in the issue thread (not just the reporter) and fixed by merged PR #1927, which I diffed directly: the new setup.py gates sm_100/sm_120 behind `bare_metal_version >= Version('12.8')` inside an outer `>= Version('12.4')` check, falling back to a plain compute_90 target below that. Rejected for expressibility: traincheck has no signal at all for whether a job builds/uses apex, let alone its version - same gap noted for megatron/transformers/deepspeed/accelerate elsewhere in this file. Also worth flagging as lowest mining priority per PLAN.md: this is a source-build failure (install-time), not a runtime training bug, so it's more of an environment-build check than the config-coherence checks traincheck otherwise focuses on.

symptom: LayerNorm/RMSNorm slower on CUDA Toolkit 13.1+ than on CUDA 13.0 Update 2, on B200/H200/L40S/A100
trigger_field: cuda_version + gpu_type
notes: PROMOTED to rules/version_incompat.py as CUDNN-LAYERNORM-001. cuDNN 9.24.0's own current Known Issues list (fetched directly, not from search snippets) states this verbatim, unconditionally on cuda_version + gpu_type - unlike the H100/Hopper candidates elsewhere in this file, NVIDIA's own text doesn't name any additional untrackable precondition (no MPS/topology/PTX caveat), so both sides of the trigger are real, already-populated JobSpec fields with no gap. A second, adjacent known issue on the same page ('cuBLASLt matmul slower on CUDA Toolkit 13.2 Update 1 vs earlier 13.x, on B200/B300') was also confirmed on the same fetch but not recorded separately since it's a strict subset/variant of the same family - revisit if promoting this one. Capped at medium confidence (not high) because it's marked 'fix planned for a future release' - no upper bound/fixed_in exists yet, consistent with the 'still open, no fix' confidence cap. Note this is a WARN-grade PERFORMANCE regression (training still completes correctly, just slower), not a correctness/crash bug - if promoted, message should say so plainly so it doesn't read as more severe than it is. cuDNN itself is bundled invisibly inside most PyTorch wheels (no cudnn_version field exists or is needed here since NVIDIA's own trigger doesn't reference it). PROMPT-B: re-fetched the cuDNN 9.24.0 backend release notes directly, exact wording and all 4 named GPUs (B200/H200/L40S/A100) re-confirmed, 'fix planned for a future release' re-confirmed (no fixed_in). Nothing to falsify - it's a current, dated NVIDIA-authored known-issues page, not a third-party incident report. Confidence remains medium (open, no fix). Verified.

symptom: No official PyTorch wheel for CUDA below 12.6 once torch is 2.8.0 or later
trigger_field: framework_version (torch) + cuda_version
notes: Fetched the raw page directly (not a summarized fetch) and confirmed the exact pip install index-url lines for 4 consecutive releases: torch 2.7.0 and 2.7.1 both ship cu118 wheels (alongside cu126/cu128); torch 2.8.0, 2.9.0, and 2.10.0 do not - their wheel index only offers cu126/cu128/cu129(2.8 only)/cu130(2.9+). This is a first-party, unambiguous vendor requirement (not an incident report) so it doesn't need the usual falsification search - the absence of a cu118 index-url line across 3 consecutive later releases is the fact itself. Not a 'necessary but not sufficient' case like the Hopper-specific rules elsewhere in this file - if the config truly specifies torch>=2.8.0 with cuda<12.6, no matching official wheel exists, full stop. Caveat: framework_version is single-purpose (whichever one framework a JobSpec names), so this assumes the 'framework' block actually refers to PyTorch, not some other named framework - same caveat already accepted for MEGATRON-DCP-001. In practice framework_version is populated from PYTORCH_VERSION/NVIDIA_PYTORCH_VERSION env vars via image extraction in most adapters, so it reliably is torch's version whenever it's set that way.

symptom: LearningRateFinder UnpicklingError from torch.load defaulting weights_only=True since torch 2.6

fixed_in.version: merged to main via PR #21758 (2026-06-09); no tagged release since 2.6.5 (2026-05-27) confirmed to contain it yet

trigger_field: framework_version (torch) + pytorch-lightning package version (not tracked) + LearningRateFinder callback usage (not tracked)
notes: Real, maintainer-merged fix: torch>=2.6 flipped torch.load's weights_only default to True, and Lightning's LearningRateFinder callback restores a temp checkpoint containing full module objects (not just weights) without passing weights_only=False, causing an UnpicklingError. Confirmed via the merged PR (#21758) description matching the issue exactly. Confidence capped at medium: fix not yet in any tagged pytorch-lightning release as of mining date. Rejected for expressibility: traincheck has no pytorch-lightning package-version field (not in extractors/lockfile.py's tracked-package set) AND no way to detect that a training script specifically uses the LearningRateFinder callback (a runtime API choice, invisible to static config/launch-flag reading). Both gaps would need solving, and the second one may not be solvable without executing code - similar to the NCCL profiler-plugin-usage gap already documented.

sides.version_range: <2.6.4 (approximate - fix merged 2026-03-17, oldest confirmed-containing tag is 2.6.4 from 2026-05-20)

symptom: Fabric FSDP bf16-mixed precision initializes parameters in bf16 instead of documented fp32
trigger_field: mixed_precision + pytorch-lightning package version (not tracked) + Lightning Fabric usage specifically (not tracked, distinct from the regular Trainer)
notes: Real bug with a linked source-line citation (fsdp.py's module_init_context using param_dtype directly) and a merged fix PR (#21586, 'fix: FSDP mixed precision initializes params in fp32, not bf16/fp16') - confirmed the PR title/description matches the issue's root-cause claim exactly. Confidence capped at medium: didn't independently narrow whether the fix first shipped in 2.6.2/2.6.3 (not checked) or only by 2.6.4 - the fix predates both of those tags' likely dates but this wasn't directly confirmed by fetching their contents. Rejected for expressibility: same 'pytorch-lightning package version not tracked' gap as lightning-21757-weightsonly-lrfinder above, compounded here by needing to distinguish 'uses Lightning Fabric' from 'uses the regular Lightning Trainer' - not something traincheck can detect from config/launch flags alone.

symptom: Flex Attention kernels non-deterministic illegal memory access on THOR/H100/H200
trigger_field: gpu_type + NGC container release tag (not tracked - only the framework/cuda/nccl versions baked *inside* the image are read, not the container's own monthly release number)
notes: Confirmed this known issue is verbatim-absent from the 25.10 release notes and verbatim-present starting at 25.12, still listed unresolved as of the latest container checked (26.06 - i.e. still open across at least 7 months of monthly releases). A genuine, authoritative, silent-correctness bug ('illegal memory access', not just a crash) on mainstream H100/H200 training GPUs, not just the niche THOR SoC - this would be a strong promotion candidate if expressible. Rejected purely on a structural field-gap: traincheck extracts cuda_version/nccl_version/framework_version from env vars baked *inside* an NGC image (CUDA_VERSION, NCCL_VERSION, NVIDIA_PYTORCH_VERSION), but never reads the container's own monthly release tag (e.g. the '25.12' in nvcr.io/nvidia/pytorch:25.12-py3) - and NVIDIA's own known-issues are indexed BY that release number, not by the framework version inside it. This is a new, distinct field-gap from anything found earlier this session - flagging it as its own line in PLAN.md's field-gaps list, since it blocks this AND the NVSHMEM finding below.

symptom: NVSHMEM segfault unless NVIDIA_IMEX_CHANNELS=0 is set when launching the container
trigger_field: NGC container release tag (not tracked) + NVIDIA_IMEX_CHANNELS comm_env var (tracked, but the container-version half of the trigger is missing)
notes: Confirmed verbatim: 25.10's own release notes say 'NVSHMEM shipped with 25.10 might require setting NVIDIA_IMEX_CHANNELS=0...', and 25.12/26.06 both still list it (now worded '25.10 and later'), so still open/unresolved. Interesting case: comm_env already tracks arbitrary NCCL/CUDA-related env vars generically (see build_comm_env), so IF the container-release-tag field existed, half of this trigger (checking whether NVIDIA_IMEX_CHANNELS=0 is actually set) would already be free - only the container-tag half is the real gap. Same NGC-container-tag field-gap as ngc-pytorch-2512-flexattention-nondeterministic-oob above; recording both confirms this is a recurring, multi-instance gap worth prioritizing if NGC mining continues.

symptom: Ampere-style MMA (mma shape 16x8x128) miscompiled on SM80/SM90/SM100, causing illegal memory access
trigger_field: cuda_version + gpu_type
notes: Fetched 12.9.0's raw page text directly (not summarized) and confirmed verbatim: 'Starting with CUDA 12.8, we observed miscompilation issues with kernels that use Ampere-style MMA (for example: mma.m16n8k16 for FP16), specifically with MMA shape 16x8x128... affects precisions including fp8_e5m2, fp8_e4m3, fp16, bf16, and int8, when performing GEMM operations where any of the M, N, or K dimensions are not divisible by 16. The miscompiled code can result in runtime errors such as illegal memory access on SM80, SM90, and SM100 architectures... actively working to fix this issue in an upcoming release.' Also confirmed this text is verbatim ABSENT from 12.8.0's own release notes (grepped raw HTML directly) despite being introduced there - only documented starting at 12.9.0, so 12.8.0 shipped the bug silently undocumented. SM80/SM90/SM100 = A100/H100/Blackwell (B100/B200/GB200) - unusually broad GPU coverage for a single known issue. Did not confirm current status against the newest CUDA 13.3 docs (NVIDIA restructured cuda-toolkit-release-notes into per-topic sub-pages rather than one cumulative archive page for the 13.x series, and a quick fetch didn't surface a clear Resolved Issues entry either way) - kept confidence at medium and left the range open-ended rather than assume it's fixed. Like the other silent-corruption Hopper/CUDA candidates in this file, the checkable trigger (cuda_version + gpu_type) is necessary but not sufficient - the actual failure additionally requires a custom kernel using this specific MMA shape with non-16-divisible dimensions, which traincheck cannot detect; promote as WARN with that precondition spelled out, same pattern as NCCL-H100-001.
